name: Alexi Giannoulias
bio: Alexi Giannoulias has served as Illinois Secretary of State since January 2023, elected in November 2022. He previously served as Illinois State Treasurer from 2007 to 2011. In 2010, he ran for the U.S. Senate seat vacated by Barack Obama, losing to Republican Mark Kirk by 2 percentage points. His family owned Broadway Bank in Chicago, which failed in April 2010 during the financial crisis — an issue that dogged his 2010 Senate campaign. As Secretary of State, he has focused on driver's license access, voting rights, and digitizing state services.
prior_office: Illinois Secretary of State (2023–present); Illinois State Treasurer (2007–2011)

jacket.note: Running uncontested in Democratic primary. Finance data for Secretary of State race pending ILSBE lookup.

red_flags.title: Broadway Bank failure — family bank collapsed during 2010 financial crisis
red_flags.description: Broadway Bank, owned by the Giannoulias family, was seized by federal regulators and failed in April 2010. The bank had made loans to convicted criminals including Tony Rezko and Michael 'Jaws' Giorango, and had expanded its risky loan portfolio significantly under Alexi's tenure as senior loan officer (2002-2006) before he became State Treasurer. The failure became a major issue in his 2010 U.S. Senate race.

news_hits.title: 2026 Illinois Primary Live Results
news_hits.summary: 270toWin election day results tracker confirms Alexi Giannoulias is unopposed in the Democratic primary for his second term as Illinois Secretary of State.
